annotations.label: Koch snowflake fractal dimension

anchor.snippet: One non-trivial example is the fractal dimension of a Koch snowflake

annotations.note: Mathlib mentions the von Koch snowflake only in a comment in `Mathlib.Topology.MetricSpace.Snowflaking`; no theorem computes its fractal dimension.

annotations.label: Fractal dimension

anchor.snippet: A fractal dimension is an index for characterizing fractal patterns or sets by quantifying their complexity as a ratio of the change in detail to the change in scale

annotations.note: Mathlib has no abstract notion of "fractal dimension" but does formalize a concrete instance, the Hausdorff dimension `MeasureTheory.dimH`.

annotations.label: Fractal vs topological dimension criterion

anchor.snippet: If the theoretical fractal dimension of a set exceeds its topological dimension, the set is considered to have fractal geometry

annotations.note: Mathlib has `smallInductiveDimension` and `dimH`, but no comparison criterion of this kind.

annotations.label: Scaling relationship equation

anchor.snippet: Such familiar scaling relationships obey equation

annotations.note: No grep hits for the scaling relation N = ε^(−D) as a formal equation.

annotations.label: Line and square scaling examples

anchor.snippet: In the line example, the dimension

annotations.note: These illustrative scaling computations for a line and square are not formalized.

annotations.label: Fractal dimension as power of scaling factor

anchor.snippet: Fractal dimension generalizes traditional dimension in that it can be fractional

annotations.note: No general scaling-exponent definition of fractal dimension is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Koch snowflake D value

anchor.snippet: For example, the Koch snowflake has

annotations.note: The Koch snowflake dimension value log 4 / log 3 is mentioned only in a docstring comment in `Snowflaking.lean`, not proved.

annotations.label: D is not a unique descriptor

anchor.section: D is not a unique descriptor
anchor.snippet: fractal dimensions are general descriptors that do not uniquely define patterns

annotations.note: Informal commentary; not the kind of statement formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Box-counting dimension

anchor.snippet: Box-counting dimension is estimated as the exponent of a power law

annotations.note: No box-counting / Minkowski–Bouligand dimension files exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Information dimension

anchor.snippet: Information dimension considers how the average information needed to identify an occupied box scales with box size

annotations.note: Information dimension is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Correlation dimension
annotations.note: Correlation dimension is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Higuchi dimension
annotations.note: Higuchi dimension is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multifractal dimensions

anchor.snippet: Multifractal dimensions: a special case of Rényi dimensions where scaling behaviour varies in different parts of the pattern

annotations.note: No multifractal-spectrum formalization exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hausdorff dimension

anchor.snippet: The Hausdorff dimension of S is defined by

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.dimH s` is defined and `dimH_def` shows it equals `⨆ d, (μH[d] s = ∞) → d`, matching the Wikipedia definition.

anchors.snippet: The Hausdorff dimension of S is defined by

anchors.value: {\displaystyle C_{H}^{d}(S):=\inf {\Bigl \{}\sum _{i}r_{i}^{d}:{\text{ there is a cover of }}S{\text{ by balls with radii }}r_{i}>0{\Bigr \}}.}

annotations.label: Hausdorff content

anchor.snippet: the d -dimensional Hausdorff content of S is defined by

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the pre-measure `mkMetric'.pre m r` (which specializes to the Hausdorff content at radius r) and uses it to build `hausdorffMeasure`, but does not name the Hausdorff content as a separate notion.

anchors.snippet: the d -dimensional Hausdorff content of S is defined by

anchors.value: {\displaystyle C_{H}^{d}(S):=\inf {\Bigl \{}\sum _{i}r_{i}^{d}:{\text{ there is a cover of }}S{\text{ by balls with radii }}r_{i}>0{\Bigr \}}.}
